game_name: Wizardry V: Heart of the Maelstrom

danger_signals.visual_form: Unnatural energies manifesting in dungeon environment
danger_signals.meaning: World-state corruption: chaotic forces have breached into the material plane, destabilizing the order maintained by L'Kbreth's Orb

danger_signals.visual_form: Tunnels beneath Temple of Sages characterized by chaotic convergence
danger_signals.meaning: Focal point of danger; spatial manifestation of world-state threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Rogue sorceress and her adventuring party as hostile encounter
danger_signals.meaning: Political/factional danger: imprisoned the Gatekeeper, represents opposition to world restoration
World-state vulnerability marker: seal mechanism is offline/unavailable

safety_signals.visual_form: Aboveground temple structure serving as entry point
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary/hub; institutional order and knowledge center

safety_signals.visual_form: High priest NPC guardian of L'Kbreth's Orb
safety_signals.meaning: Trustworthy authority figure; represents continuity of protective order

safety_signals.visual_form: Artifact of power maintained by priesthood
safety_signals.meaning: Symbol of world stability; when functional, peace is maintained
Restoration artifact; obtaining it signals successful world-state stabilization

faction_indicators.faction: Priesthood of Llylgamyn / Order of Sages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temple of Sages aboveground structure; deep priesthood presence in lower caverns (G'bli Gedook)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rogue sorceress identity; her adventuring party
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile party of adventurers accompanying the Sorn
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Deep Maelstrom locations; imprisonment site of Gatekeeper; appears as final boss encounter

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Four suit-based entities (implied card suits: Hearts, Diamonds, Clubs, Spades)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Located on 8th floor of dungeon; require appeasement via suit-specific objects

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Seal mechanism personified; represents neutral/cosmic order force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imprisoned in Heart of the Maelstrom; summoned after Card Lords are appeased

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: L'Kbreth's Orb functioning; Temple of Sages stable; orderly surface world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No hostile encounters; normal activity maintained

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unnatural energies visually manifest in Maelstrom tunnels; environmental corruption visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Adventurers recruited; monsters encountered; dungeon becomes hostile; repeated re-entry required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression deeper into dungeon becomes possible; Heart of Maelstrom becomes accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Path to Gatekeeper opens; summoning ritual becomes possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sorn's party appears as visual/tactical encounter in deep Maelstrom
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final battle triggers; world restoration hinges on victory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gatekeeper grants Heart of Abriel; return to surface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Order restored to Llylgamyn; game objective complete

environmental_storytelling.example: Dungeon depth (floor numbers) correlates with story progress: shallow floors = exposition via G'bli Gedook; 8th floor = Card Lords threshold; Heart = climax
L'Kbreth's Orb represents previous peace; its loss of efficacy is the inciting incident; Heart of Abriel represents restoration
High priest G'bli Gedook stationed in early dungeon floors acts as guide/expositor; establishes priesthood as legitimate order faction
Non-hostile monsters (noted as improvement by GamePro) suggest nuanced world state; not all dungeon entities are enemies; some are neutral or conditional
Card Lords requiring suit-specific offerings implies hidden power structure beneath chaos; solving puzzles = understanding world mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Instructs party to venture deeper; provides objective clarity; guards L'Kbreth's Orb
Require specific suits as offerings; only allow progression upon satisfaction; not inherently hostile
Strikes during climactic moment; imprisons Gatekeeper; leads hostile party; final combat encounter
Summoned after Card Lords appeased; grants reward upon Sorn's defeat; enacts final world stabilization
Recruited for dungeon exploration; navigate repeated re-entry (gameplay requirement); resolve chaos by defeating Sorn
Visually manifest in Maelstrom; concentrated in tunnel system; physically emergent threat requiring sealing

notes: Wizardry V communicates world-state primarily through narrative structure mapped to spatial progression. The game contrasts a pre-chaos peace (L'Kbreth's Orb functional) with an emergent crisis (unnatural energies) that requires restoring a broken seal mechanism (the imprisoned Gatekeeper). Factions are defined by their relationship to this central conflict: the Priesthood maintains order, the Sorn represents chaos/opposition, and the Card Lords are neutral gatekeepers. Environmental storytelling relies on dungeon depth as narrative pacing and NPC positioning as authority markers. The introduction of non-hostile monsters (noted as improvement over Wizardry IV) suggests a more nuanced world-state where entities have variable alignment rather than binary hostility. The game's core manifestation pattern is: chaotic emergence → guided quest → factional opposition → seal restoration → world reordering.
